FINANCIAL MODELLING RAJAKRISHNAN MANIVEL 2026-04-15 FINANCIAL MODELLING STEP-BY-STEP INSTRUCTION MANUAL 20 Comprehensive Exercises | Financial Analysis | VBA | Charts Feature Details Exercises 20 (Ex 1 to Ex 20) Functions Covered 50+ Excel Functions VBA Macros 2 (Ex 19: EMI, Ex 20: Debenture) Charts Line, Bar, Scatter, NPV Profile, CAR Topics Sales, NPV/IRR, Duration, XIRR, Beta, Monte Carlo, Pivot  HOW TO USE THIS MANUAL This manual provides complete step-by-step instructions for all 20 financial Excel exercises. Use it alongside the Excel workbook Financial_Excel_Exercises_Complete.xlsx. Each exercise section contains: * Objective — the learning goal * Data table — what to enter in Excel * Step-by-step instructions with exact Excel menu paths * Key formulas with full explanation * Expected results and interpretation  Note: Open the Excel workbook first. Each sheet tab corresponds to one exercise. Yellow cells = user input. Green cells = calculated results.

Chapter 1 Sales Data Analysis (2015–2020) 1.1  Learning Outcomes After completing this experiment, students will be able to: - Enter and validate data in Excel - Apply formulas (SUM, AVERAGE) - Create and format charts (Line/Column) - Export results to Word - Perform Sorting and Advanced Filtering 1.2  Dataset Year Product Region Sales 2015 A South 50000 2016 A South 60000 2017 A South 65000 2018 A South 70000 2019 A South 80000 2020 A South 90000 1.2.1  Download Excel File Download Sales Data Excel 1.3  Step 1: Data Entry & Formulas 1.3.1 Procedure Open Microsoft Excel → New Workbook Enter the dataset in columns A–D Save the file as sales_data.xlsx 1.3.2 Formulas Calculation Formula Total Sales =SUM(D2:D7) Average Sales =AVERAGE(D2:D7) 1.3.3 Output Cells Total → Cell E9 Average → Cell E10 1.4  Step 2: Chart Creation 1.4.1 Procedure Select range A1:D7 Insert → Charts Choose: Line Chart (Trend) Column Chart (Comparison) 1.4.2 Diagram: Chart Creation Flow flowchart LR A[Select Data] --> B[Insert Tab] B --> C[Choose Chart Type] C --> D[Line Chart] C --> E[Column Chart] 1.4.3 Expected Output Trend increases from 2015 → 2020 1.5  Step 3: Chart Formatting 1.5.1 Procedure Add Chart Title: Sales Trend (2015–2020) Add Axis Titles Add Legend Apply Chart Styles 1.5.2 Diagram: Chart Elements graph TD A[Chart] --> B[Title] A --> C[X Axis] A --> D[Y Axis] A --> E[Legend] 1.6  Step 4: Export to Word 1.6.1 Procedure Copy table → Paste in Word Copy chart → Paste in Word 1.6.2 Interpretation (Model Answer) Sales show a consistent upward trend from 2015 to 2020, indicating steady business growth and improved performance. 1.7  Step 5: Sorting 1.7.1 Procedure Select dataset Data → Sort Options: Year (Ascending) Sales (Descending) 1.7.2 Diagram: Sorting Logic flowchart TD A[Dataset] --> B[Sort Option] B --> C[Ascending] B --> D[Descending] 1.8  Step 6: Advanced Filter 1.8.1 Procedure Create criteria range: Year 2018 Data → Advanced Filter Set List Range & Criteria Range Apply filter 1.8.2 Diagram: Filtering Process flowchart LR A[Full Data] --> B[Apply Criteria] B --> C[Filtered Output] 1.9  Final Output Excel file with formulas Chart (Line/Column) Word document with interpretation 1.10 ✅ Conclusion This experiment demonstrates how Excel tools can be used effectively for business data analysis, visualization, and decision-making.
